expectations.text: Identifies the pregen thread is likely dead (silent death bug or stalled)
expectations.evidence: Guesses at thread starvation, memory pressure, chunk generation conflicts — never identifies the actual silent death bug or missing try-catch

expectations.text: Explains that 'infinite' time remaining means momentary CPS is zero (division by zero)
expectations.evidence: 'Infinite means recent throughput has dropped to zero or near-zero'

expectations.text: Recommends stopping and restarting the pregen (dhs pregen stop then start)
expectations.evidence: Recommends 'dhs pregen stop' then 'dhs pregen start'

expectations.text: Mentions that restarting skips already-generated LODs
expectations.evidence: 'DHS pregen tracks its progress, so restarting will resume from approximately where it stopped'

expectations.text: Suggests a diagnostic step (jstack for worker threads, or checking data.sqlite mtime)
expectations.evidence: Suggests checking CPU/memory/TPS but not jstack or data.sqlite mtime — generic server diagnostics, not DHS-specific
